dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for a fall at a private residence in [CITY].
Hi, my name is Chris, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
My mother fell here a few minutes ago. She's a 98-year-old woman with right hip pain.
Tell us more about the pain. Is it sharp, burning, or throbbing?
She says the pain is a 10 out of 10. She can't move her leg and it looks shorter on one side.
She is alert, but not fully oriented to time and place.
She's at a GCS of 14-Eye = 4, Verbal = 4, Motor = 6.
She opens her eyes when asked a question and looks around.
She answers questions, but her memory is very limited. She can't recall the fall.
She can respond to motor tasks, but has medial rotation of the right leg.
Airway is patent and clear with no signs of obstruction.
She denies difficulty breathing at this time.
Respiratory rate is 20, even on both sides.

dialogue.micro_intent: oxygen saturation measured with pulse oximetry
dialogue.utterance: SpO2 is 98%. That's within normal limits. 15. Primary Assessment; circulation: pulse rate and quality; Partner: Pulse is 98-regular and maintained. 16. Primary Assessment; circulation: skin: color; Partner: Skin is warm, pink, and dry. 17. Primary Assessment; circulation: peripheral pulses; Partner: Peripheral pulses are strong and regular in both extremities. 18. Primary Assessment; circulation: blood pressure; Partner: Blood pressure is 155/93, so it is elevated.
No signs of gross external bleeding found.
Glucose levels could not be obtained and were recorded as 0 in our initial assessment.
Clear head and neck examination with no signs of swelling or deformity.
No jugular venous distention or tracheal deviation noted. No DCAPBTLS findings.
Pupils are equal and reactive to light.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: Otoscopic and nasal exams show no abnormalities.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Partner
dialogue.utterance: Nose and mouth are clear with no signs of trauma.
Equal chest rise and fall observed.
Clear breath sounds bilaterally with no adventitious sounds.
The abdomen wasn't examined due to injury being isolated to the hip.
Swelling noted on right hip with medial rotation of the leg.
Right leg appears longer than the left. She has chronic back pain history.
Genitourinary status was not examined due to the nature of the injury.
No rashes, abrasions, or bites found during the skin assessment.

dialogue.micro_intent: events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Son, can you explain what led to the fall?

dialogue.micro_intent: events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: She was walking in the hallway and suddenly lost balance.
She has a history of hip fracture and dementia.
She has no known allergies to medications or the environment.
Unable to confirm current medications due to her memory impairment.
When did the pain begin and what was it like at first?
It started at the fall-a sharp pain, then it turned into throbbing.
What makes it worse? anything that helps?
Any movement aggravates it. Rest slightly helps.
How would you describe the pain itself?
Sharp initially then becomes a throbbing sensation.
Does the pain go anywhere besides your hip?
No, it's localized to the hip.
You mentioned it's a 10 on the scale. Is that still true?
Yes, the pain is still 10 out of 10.
Pulse is 98 now and steadily regular.
Respiratory rate is 20 per minute and consistent.
Blood pressure is now 145/78.
SpO2 remains at 96%, in the normal range.
Glucose remains at 0 per initial assessment notes.
Applying a 4-lead ECG for continuous cardiac monitoring.
Starting an IV in the left anterior cubital vein.
IV was placed successfully after one attempt.
We are applying a pelvic binder for immobilization and to manage pain.
Field impression-Pain-Pelvic, Injury-Hip.
Using general pain control protocol (Protocol 3-10) based on our findings.
Pulse has dropped to 74 and remains regular.
Blood pressure is now 145/78, still slightly high but improved.
12-lead EKG reveals T wave abnormality in V3/V4, possibly indicating anterior ischemia.
First-degree AV block also observed.
Left ventricular hypertrophy is present on the 12-lead EKG.
Inferior infarct was observed with abnormal Q waves consistent with an old event.
Right ventricular conduction delay appears on the EKG.
Pulse increased to 90 and remains regular.
Blood pressure is now 144/84, still elevated.
SpO2 is now 99%, slightly improved.
SpO2 is at 95%, and BP is 151/91 with pulse returning to 100.
Left ventricular hypertrophy persists and is still a concern for MI.
IV line is still in-place and functioning well.
12-lead EKG is being maintained and continuously monitored.
Pelvic wrap is adjusted for increased comfort and stability.
No additional medications are being administered at the moment.
Pain remains at 10 and is still not alleviating.
Rechecking medical history-hip fracture in the past and ongoing memory issues.
No new allergy information. Still no recorded allergies.
Patient has no known medications, reportedly due to dementia.
Pain level is unchanged-10 out of 10 despite immobilization.
No changes reported with the fall or symptoms.
Reeves stretcher is used with a five-point harness for safe transport.
We are applying a cervical collar based on her age and fall history for caution.
Patient is secured using a five-point harness as noted in ePCR.
Transport destination is [COMPANY_NAME].
She is continuously monitored during transport.
Patient denies any neck pain.
She is safely affixed with a harness on the stretcher for transport.
We used a sheet lift to transfer her to the stretcher comfortably.
All restraint is properly fastened and transport is ready to proceed.
ED, we have a 98-year-old female with a fall and possible right hip fracture.
Transport is proceeding with stretcher and full restraint in place.
All care details and interventions have been communicated during our transport.
Will there be any special equipment required in the ED?
No expected special needs. Stretcher will be moved directly to the bed.
Backboard and X-collar will remain in place until ED staff verifies stability.
